source_pr.title: planner: add usage of paging copr in optimizer
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #30578

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

Add the usage of paging copr in the optimizer, when `tidb_enable_paging` is on, the optimizer will choose whether go through paging for double read case.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/9587680/145348653-88dcaeee-7d23-4922-96c5-e51423268aba.png)

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
Speedup indexlookup by paging copr.
```

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("explain format='brief' select * from t force index(i) where id <= 99 and c1 >= 0 and c1 <= 99 and c2 in (2, 4, 6, 8) order by c1 limit 1").Check(kit.Rows( (planner/core/plan_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("explain format='brief' select * from t force index(i) where id <= 99 and c1 >= 0 and c1 <= 99 and c2 in (2, 4, 6, 8) order by c1 limit 1").Check(kit.Rows( (planner/core/plan_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: tk.MustQuery("explain format='brief' select * from t force index(i) where id <= 99 and c1 >= 0 and c1 <= 99 and c2 in (2, 4, 6, 8) order by c1 limit 1").Check(kit.Rows( (planner/core/plan_test.go).
fix_pattern: tk.MustQuery("explain format='brief' select * from t force index(i) where id <= 99 and c1 >= 0 and c1 <= 99 and c2 in (2, 4, 6, 8) order by c1 limit 1").Check(kit.Rows( (planner/core/plan_test.go)

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("explain format='brief' select * from t force index(i) where id <= 99 and c1 >= 0 and c1 <= 99 and c2 in (2, 4, 6, 8) order by c1 limit 1").Check(kit.Rows( (planner/core/plan_test.go)
